title: Malvastrum spiciflorum
taxonomy: Malvastrum spiciflorum belongs to the family Malvaceae and is classified within the genus Malvastrum. It is a member of the Malvales order and is often found in the southern United States. This plant is a type of shrub or small tree, commonly known as the spiciflorum malvastrum or the Mexican hollyhock. The Malvastrum spiciflorum is native to Mexico and parts of Central America. It is a species that is often used in horticulture for its showy flowers and attractive foliage.

intro: Malvastrum spiciflorum is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Malvaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions, including wound healing and respiratory issues.
